question: [File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/RecordFileService.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: findByBlockType(BlockType block)] Can an unprivileged external user send a block value of '0x0' to BlockType.of(), which creates BlockType(name='0x0', number=0) that fails the identity check against BlockType.EARLIEST (name='earliest', number=0), routing to findByIndex(0) and bypassing the permanent earliest-block cache, and by sending this repeatedly, generate continuous DB queries for the genesis block that degrade overall service performance?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/RecordFileService.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: findByTimestamp(Long timestamp)] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with timestamps that are exactly at the boundary between two record files (consensusEnd of one file equals consensusStart of the next), causing findByTimestamp() to return an ambiguous or incorrect record file due to the '>= ?1' predicate, and if this incorrect result is cached, cause all subsequent requests with that timestamp to receive wrong block context, disrupting EVM execution?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/RecordFileService.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: findByBlockType(BlockType block)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the 10-minute expiry of the recordFileIndex cache (CACHE_MANAGER_RECORD_FILE_INDEX, expireAfterWrite=10min) by sending requests for the same block index repeatedly just after cache expiry, causing a sustained pattern of DB queries every 10 minutes that, when multiplied across many unique block indices, creates a periodic DB load spike that halts transaction confirmation?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/RecordFileService.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: findByTimestamp(Long timestamp)] Can an unprivileged external user send a timestamp equal to the consensusEnd of the latest record file to findByTimestamp(), causing the query to return the latest record file and cache it under that timestamp key, and then by sending the same timestamp repeatedly after the 10-minute expireAfterAccess window, force repeated DB queries that compete with legitimate transaction confirmation queries?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/RecordFileService.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: findByBlockType(BlockType block)] Can an unprivileged external user send a block value containing only whitespace or special characters to BlockType.of(), causing StringUtils.isEmpty() to return false (since it checks for null/empty but not blank), leading extractNumericBlock() to attempt Long.parseLong() on the whitespace string, throwing a NumberFormatException that if unhandled propagates up the call stack and crashes the request thread?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/RecordFileService.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: findByBlockType(BlockType block)] Can an unprivileged external user send a block value of 'LATEST' (uppercase) to BlockType.of(), which converts to lowercase 'latest' and returns BlockType.LATEST singleton, but if there is any inconsistency in the toLowerCase() conversion for non-ASCII characters in the block string, cause an unexpected BlockType to be created that bypasses the identity check and routes to findByIndex() with an incorrect number?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/RecordFileService.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: findByTimestamp(Long timestamp)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that findByTimestamp() uses 'consensusEnd >= ?1' (not an exact match) to send a timestamp slightly before a gap in the record file sequence, causing the query to return the next record file after the gap, and if this incorrect mapping is cached, cause all EVM executions referencing that timestamp to use wrong block context, breaking transaction confirmation logic?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/RecordFileService.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: findByBlockType(BlockType block)] Can an unprivileged external user send a block value that is a valid hex number but represents a block index that does not exist in the database (e.g., '0xdeadbeef'), causing findByIndex() to return empty (uncached due to unless='#result == null'), and by sending thousands of such unique hex values per second, exhaust the database connection pool and prevent legitimate transaction confirmation queries from executing?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/RecordFileService.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: findByTimestamp(Long timestamp)] Can an unprivileged external user send a timestamp of 1 (the smallest positive value) to findByTimestamp(), causing the query to return the genesis record file (if consensusEnd >= 1), cache it under key 1, and then by sending timestamps 1, 2, 3, ... up to 10000 in rapid succession, fill the recordFileTimestamp cache with entries all pointing to the genesis block, poisoning the cache for legitimate timestamp lookups?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/RecordFileService.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: findByBlockType(BlockType block)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of input validation on block number size in BlockType.of() to send extremely large decimal strings (e.g., 19-digit numbers near Long.MAX_VALUE) that parse successfully but reference non-existent block indices, causing findByIndex() to always miss the cache and hit the database, enabling a sustained DoS attack against transaction confirmation?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/RecordFileService.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: findByTimestamp(Long timestamp)] Can an unprivileged external user send concurrent requests with the same timestamp value that is not yet cached, triggering multiple simultaneous DB queries for 'select r from RecordFile r where r.consensusEnd >= ?1' before the first result is cached, creating a thundering herd on the database that, when repeated across many unique timestamps, exhausts DB connections and halts transaction confirmation?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/RecordFileService.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: findByBlockType(BlockType block)] Can an unprivileged external user send a block value of '-0x1' to BlockType.of(), where the hex prefix is detected first but the cleaned value '-1' still contains the NEGATIVE_NUMBER_PREFIX '-', causing an IllegalArgumentException to be thrown, and if this exception is not rate-limited or properly handled, flood the service with such requests to exhaust error-handling resources and degrade transaction confirmation?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/RecordFileService.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: findByBlockType(BlockType block)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the @Primary annotation on cacheManagerRecordFileIndex bean to interfere with other cache managers that depend on the primary bean, causing cache resolution conflicts that result in incorrect cache lookups for findByIndex() and findByTimestamp(), returning stale or wrong RecordFile objects that break EVM execution context?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/RecordFileService.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: findByTimestamp(Long timestamp)] Can an unprivileged external user send a timestamp that falls in a gap between two record files (where no record file has consensusEnd >= timestamp but less than the next file's consensusStart), causing findByTimestamp() to return the wrong record file (the next one after the gap), and if this is used for EVM execution, cause transactions to be evaluated against incorrect block state, disrupting confirmation?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/RecordFileService.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: findByBlockType(BlockType block)] Can an unprivileged external user send a block value of '0x' followed by a very long hex string (e.g., 100 hex characters) to BlockType.of(), causing Long.parseLong() to throw a NumberFormatException due to overflow, and if the exception handling in extractNumericBlock() re-throws as IllegalArgumentException without proper logging throttling, flood the log system with error messages that consume I/O resources and degrade service performance?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/RecordFileService.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: findByTimestamp(Long timestamp)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the expireAfterAccess=10min policy of the recordFileTimestamp cache by sending a request for a specific timestamp once every 9 minutes to keep it alive in the cache indefinitely, while simultaneously sending requests for 9999 other timestamps to fill the cache, preventing legitimate timestamp entries from being cached and forcing DB queries for all other requests?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/RecordFileService.java -> Scope: Critical: Network not being able to confirm new transactions (total network shutdown)'] [Function: findByBlockType(BlockType block)] Can an unprivileged external user send a block value of '0x00000000000000000' (hex zero with many leading zeros) to BlockType.of(), which after removing the '0x' prefix leaves '00000000000000000', and Long.parseLong('00000000000000000', 16) successfully returns 0, creating BlockType(name='0x00000000000000000', number=0) that fails the identity check against EARLIEST, routing to findByIndex(0) and bypassing the permanent earliest-block cache?
